key_insights.insight: It is a matter of focus whether you focus on things you don't like or things you want to celebrate about yourself.
key_insights.quote: You can focus on all the things you don't like about yourself, or you can choose to, you know, accept and acknowledge that there are some things you're not as good at and mistakes you've made.

key_insights.insight: Everyone makes mistakes, but you can choose not to dwell on them constantly.
key_insights.quote: They have all made mistakes they all have ugly bits of themselves they don't like silly things they've done awful things they've said slip-ups they've made you know they've done things not from a benevolent place we've all done it but we can choose to not live in that area the whole time and like focus on it and drown in it.

key_insights.insight: Accepting your shadow side alongside celebrating your good qualities is essential for self-compassion.
key_insights.quote: You haven't got to ignore and shun the shadow side because we've all got that, but have an acceptance of it alongside celebrating the good stuff, I think is really what I learned from writing that chapter.

key_insights.insight: Workaholic behavior can stem from deep-rooted feelings of inadequacy.
key_insights.quote: Sometimes that is coming from a place of wanting to do well some of the time a lot of the time probably 80 of the time that is coming from a place of i'm a shitty person i don't deserve what i've got i don't deserve to have the job that I've got.

key_insights.insight: Actions that stem from self-compassion bring a sense of peace.
key_insights.quote: When I go into workaholic mode, I go, oh, I don't, I must be not liking myself very much. I'm beating myself up about something within me.

key_insights.insight: Practicing self-compassion requires focusing on the things you like about yourself and your gifts.
key_insights.quote: You can focus on the stuff that you really like about yourself and that you really want to celebrate about yourself and that you know and that you notice the gifts that you have because we've all got that every single person has got something to give

actionable_advice.advice: Identify personal patterns indicative of negative self-talk.
actionable_advice.how_to: Reflect on behaviors and habits to identify the ones that increase when self-doubt is present.  For example, overworking is a sign of not liking myself very much.

actionable_advice.advice: Schedule small acts of self-care to counter negative cycles.
actionable_advice.how_to: Incorporate simple activities into the routine, such as taking a walk, listening to music, or engaging in friendly conversations.

actionable_advice.advice: Confront the root cause of negative feelings.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dig down to that place of what is it that I don't like about myself.

memorable_stories: The guest recounts how conversations with a shaman, Wendy Mandy, emphasized the importance of self-compassion and how this inspired her to explore the topic in her book.
The guest describes being a workaholic to combat negative self-talk and how her husband has to force her to stop.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 52 - Why You Need To Be Kinder To Yourself: Fearne Cotton
